content.text:
*(slight shift in posture, faster)*

Oh, everything. The project at work that I've been avoiding for — honestly, months. A friendship I've been meaning to repair. The book I said I was going to write, which, *(short laugh)* classic. Exercise has been inconsistent. I had a conversation with my manager two weeks ago that I've probably replayed forty or fifty times because I said something that came out wrong and I can't tell if she noticed.

*(pauses)*

And the thing is — and I know how this sounds — the thing is it's not wrong. Like, I *am* behind. I *did* handle that conversation awkwardly. The voice isn't making things up, it's just... the volume is insane. And the timing. It doesn't bring this up when I could actually do something about it, it brings it up at two in the morning.

*(looks down briefly)*

And then I feel like I should be able to just — I know the cognitive behavioral stuff, I know how to reality-test, I know about the negativity bias, I know all of it — and I still can't make it stop. Which is its own thing the voice has opinions about, obviously.
